title: Savory Middle Eastern Lamb Bowl
description: A savory and crumbly Middle Eastern dish featuring lamb, lemon zest, and a lacquer glaze, served in a lacquerware bowl. This ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly recipe is perfect for a brunch mood.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: boneless and cut into small pieces

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the lacquer glaze, olive oil, lemon zest, sumac, cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb pieces to the bowl and toss to coat with the glaze mixture.
Spread the lamb pieces out in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Roast the lamb in the preheated oven for 20 minutes.
While the lamb is cooking, heat a large skillet over medium heat and add the chopped onion.
Cook the onion for 5 minutes, or until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional 1 minute.
To assemble the bowls, divide the cooked lamb among four lacquerware bowls.
Top the lamb with the cooked onion and garlic mixture.
